description_long: This utility pole is a tall, cylindrical structure made primarily of metal, supporting a network of electrical wires at its top. The pole features a single, robust vertical shaft with a set of two horizontal crossarms near the top, each holding multiple insulators and colored wires. The insulators are mounted at the ends of the crossarms, and the wires are strung between them, with some sagging slightly. The top of the pole is equipped with several small, colored caps or markers, likely for identification or safety. The overall design is utilitarian, with exposed hardware and a focus on structural stability. The pole is typical of those found in urban environments for electrical distribution.
description: A tall metal utility pole with two horizontal crossarms supporting multiple colored wires and insulators at the top, designed for urban electrical distribution.
description_view_0: The pole is seen from the side, showing the full height and the circular arrangement of wires and insulators at the top.
description_view_1: A narrow end-on view, highlighting the thin profile of the pole and the horizontal alignment of the crossarms and wires.
description_view_2: Another side view similar to view 0, with a clear look at the arrangement of wires and insulators from a slightly different angle.
description_view_3: End-on view from the opposite side of view 1, again emphasizing the horizontal crossarms and the vertical shaft.
attribute_arrangement_of_crossarms: Two horizontal crossarms are mounted near the top of the pole, stacked vertically with a small gap between them.
